replies.author: @n1mbusmusic606
replies.text: Have anything for solvents? Ever heard of natural deep eutectic solvents or NADES? Definitely worth checking out. Also, there is a particularly stunning polymer called pvdf hfp that if you could figure out a cheaper greener analogue would be world saving

author: @strongforce8466
text:
Nice!!  Try it on a dirty chimney window, and also a dirty oven/oven parts, that alone is a huge market.. And they use toxic products!

By the way, what does it contain??  Without revealing the formula of course

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: I've tried this on many ovens and it works really well every time. I would it imagine it would work on chimney window.  contains no harsh chemicals

replies.author: @strongforce8466
replies.text: Cool I'd be curious to try, my father use this toxic spray.. anyway we're summer soon so no need chimney, but nice to hear it works for oven too !!

author: @chris210
text: 2 questions for you Sam, 1 have you had chance to try it on plastics abs, HIPS, and urethane resins, I'm thinking for getting rid of mold release on models myself but considering how much stuff is made from some sort of plastic would be handy and 2 will you be setting up a shop so I can buy some?

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: This works on all plastics.  I will in production soon.  Email me then when I'm ready I'll contact you

author: @lisakingscott7729
text:
Got my sample Monday, many thanks, just looking for a bottle to put it in. One further idea from the vid showing upholstery cleaning, I have a floor/carpet/upholstery cleaner which requires a dilute detergent solution. I'd like to have a go making my own solution using the ecofoam powder, so I want to order more already! :)
BTW, I noticed you like the music ;)

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: I will email you tomorrow.  Loved the music ha ha

author: @godspirals
text: Big market if you can crack it. what's the unit price to make up 500ml solution , how's it go as dishwashing detergent.

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: I'm not sure about the dishwasher but I'll have a go and it's £5 a litre

replies.author: @culturewarrefugee9625
replies.text: Hi Sam.  I'm wondering, are you saying that the dry powder is £5 a litre?

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: As a ready made solution it's £5 a litre.

author: @jonathannumer5415
text: what are the plans for production?

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: I'm tying up a few loose ends.  I'm looking at bulk to begin with then take it from there

replies.author: @jonathannumer5415
replies.text: I am from the US and the idea of creating a lightweight alternative is rarely considered.... People don't even think about the cost of shipping mostly water around... I'd love to see this come to the Americas as well :) ;)

replies.author: @samanthacarter8415
replies.text: If you email me then I can keep you informed

replies.author: @psykoklown874
replies.text: Yes, a powdered product would be much better for shipping. I would think that it would be more stable as well.
